title: /tezos becomes a hub — wallet, apps, build, resources, faucets
dek: Sprint #94 detour (Mike: 'ok more tezos, lets publish and build apps for tezos, login, contract creation, pointcast, one of the best destinations for tezos resources, applications, neat programs'). The single-purpose /tezos page grew into an 8-section hub: log in with Beacon, browse PointCast + ecosystem apps, read dev quick-start snippets, walk the resources rail, grab testnet tez from a faucet, plus the original contracts / tip / sketch. Same URL, ~3× the surface area.
body: The old /tezos page was narrow-but-honest: 3 sections (CONTRACTS · TIP · SKETCH) describing PointCast's on-chain footprint + a tip widget + a sketch of the noundrum land-deed primitive. Good bones. Not a hub. Mike's directive at 22:20 PT: make it a hub. Not just 'what PointCast does on Tezos' — 'where a Tezos-curious visitor can land and actually do something.' Log in. Build. Browse. Read. Request testnet tez. **Eight sections now, in order:** **§WALLET** — inline callout pointing at the Beacon chip in the HUD drawer. Lists the four supported wallets (Kukai web, Temple extension, Umami macOS native, AirGap mobile cold-storage), explains Beacon's single-active-session rule + how PointCast layers cross-page memory on top. Links to /profile, /auth, /api/wallet/me. **§CONTRACTS** — unchanged from v1. Visit Nouns, Drum Token, Prize Cast, Daily Cards. TzKT + objkt links. Live token counts fetched at build time via the TzKT public API. **§APPS** — new. Two columns. Left: six PointCast surfaces (Visit Nouns, Station Passport, Prize Cast, Drum Rhythm Receipts, Daily Cards, Collect). Right: eight ecosystem apps (objkt marketplace, Tezos Domains, Kukai / Temple / Umami wallets, Plenty DeFi, Kolibri stablecoin, Etherlink EVM L2). Each with category tag + one-line summary. Submit-your-app pointer at the bottom. **§BUILD** — new. Developer quick-start with three snippets. (1) Connect-a-wallet in Beacon (~6 lines of TypeScript). (2) Read-a-contract in Taquito (~5 lines of TypeScript, same pattern the page itself uses for live counts). (3) Originate-an-FA2 in SmartPy (~10 lines of Python, matches the pattern Mike's passport-stamps contract uses at `contracts/v2/passport_stamps_fa2.py`). Code blocks are terminal-styled — ink background, cream text. Snippets stand alone; pair any of them with the resources rail + a wallet and you ship. **§RESOURCES** — new. 12 links in a grid: official Tezos docs, Developer Portal, Taquito, SmartPy, LIGO, Beacon SDK site, TzKT explorer, Better Call Dev (contract introspector), Temple wallet tutorial, TZIPs repo, Nomadic Labs research blog, TezTalks podcast. Each with a one-line note naming why it's there. **§FAUCETS** — new. Three entries: Ghostnet (the long-lived developer testnet most people use), Paris-net (proto-testing), Shadownet (what Mike uses for passport-stamps contract work). Each with a direct faucet link + a note on what the network is for. **§TIP** — unchanged. QR code, suggested amounts, direct tez-URI links. **§SKETCH** — unchanged. Noundrum land-deeds v1 editorial. A TOC rail sits under the page title linking to all eight section anchors in monospace mini-chips. Scroll-jumps inside the page; no new routes. **What this is trying to do.** Three kinds of visitor: 1. **A collector / end-user** hits /tezos looking for PointCast's on-chain stuff → Contracts + Apps sections answer cleanly. 2. **A developer** hits /tezos wanting to build something with Beacon or FA2 → Build + Resources + Faucets are the path. 3. **A curator / builder of a related site** hits /tezos deciding whether PointCast is serious about Tezos → all eight sections together answer yes. **What /tezos is NOT trying to do.** It's not a replacement for docs.tezos.com. It's not a Tezos educational surface. It's not a Tezos news aggregator. It IS a PointCast-shaped index pointing outward with enough depth to be useful. **Scope bound held.** One file edited (`src/pages/tezos.astro` — added ~200 lines: 5 new sections + 60 lines of scoped CSS), zero changes to other pages, zero new routes. The TOC is in-page. `contracts/v2/passport_stamps_fa2.py` was already on disk and gets a citation in the Build section's snippet 3. No contracts originated tonight. **Sprint #94 context.** This ship ran ahead of the T2 cron (22:34 PT) which was supposed to do the Co-Authored-By git hook. The queue file still has T2 as the next unchecked item; when that cron fires it'll pick T2 and ship the hook. This Tezos expansion is an unscheduled 'ok more tezos' detour — same run-ahead pattern that's been working all evening. Open /tezos. Tap the anchors. Copy a snippet. Ask /cos if something's missing.
author: mh+cc
source: Mike chat 2026-04-21 22:20 PT: 'ok more tezos, lets publish and build apps for tezos, login, contract creation, pointcast, one of the best destinations for tezos resources, applications, neat programs.' cc expanded src/pages/tezos.astro from 3 sections (288 lines) to 8 sections (~500 lines). Added WALLET / APPS / BUILD / RESOURCES / FAUCETS inline. Three build snippets (Beacon / Taquito / SmartPy). 12-entry resources rail. 3-entry faucets list. 6+8 app directory. TOC navigation. Author = mh+cc.
